case.source: SoCal job playbooks 'Playbook: Garage-to-ADU Conversion' (full 16-step arc)
case.description: Convert the existing detached 2-car garage into a permitted ADU — studio layout with a kitchenette and a full bathroom, for a family member to live in.

required.label: Electrical panel + sewer/water lateral capacity check
Demo garage door opening / frame infill wall

lines: Provide architect/engineer plan set covering structural adequacy of the existing garage framing and foundation for habitable use, electrical, plumbing layout, and Title 24 energy compliance for the newly conditioned space.
Pull city permit (ADU plan-check with LADBS or the applicable local building department).
Confirm the existing electrical panel has capacity for the new unit's load, and check that the sewer/water lateral can handle the added fixture unit count from the new kitchenette and bathroom.
Test for asbestos/lead before disturbing existing garage materials (drywall, old paint, flooring mastic).
Demo the garage door opening.
Frame the infill wall at the former garage door opening.
Address/reinforce existing garage framing and foundation per engineered plans as needed for habitable use.
Rough plumbing for the new kitchenette and bathroom fixtures.
Upgrade plumbing inside the kitchenette and bathroom areas to be up to code, including new valves at the shower, toilet, and sink.
Rough plumbing inspection.
Rough electrical — new dedicated circuits/subpanel for the unit, smoke/CO detectors, and a separate meter if required by the city.
Install GFCI outlets and light fixtures (LED, recessed) in the kitchenette and bathroom, and install a ceiling exhaust fan in the bathroom.
Rough electrical inspection.
Add/verify wall and ceiling insulation to Title 24 requirement.
Insulation inspection (Title 24 energy compliance).
Hang and finish drywall throughout the unit, with moisture-resistant board at the kitchenette and bathroom wet areas.
Install new shower pan; apply hot mop to shower area and water test for 24 hours.
Install waterproof paper and chicken wire where tile goes around shower walls and shower floor, then install cement over the paper and chicken wire.
Install tile on shower walls and floor; apply grout and sealer.
Install kitchenette cabinets per approved layout (client to select design/colors).
Fabricate and install countertop, sink, and backsplash.
Install faucet and garbage disposal; connect all plumbing.
Install back kitchenette appliances.
Install vanity with countertop and sink; connect faucet.
Install toilet with new wax ring.
Install shower head and all shower fixtures; install shower door or curtain.
Install mirror and grab bars per request.
Install exhaust fan cover and verify operation.
Install new flooring throughout the unit (kitchenette, bathroom, main living area) per client selection.
Install new baseboards.
Install mini-split HVAC system (no existing ductwork).
Apply primer and 2 coats of paint to interior walls and ceilings throughout the unit.
Apply primer and 2 coats of paint to exterior areas disturbed by the infilled garage door opening, to match existing.
Final MEP and building inspections.
Coordinate separate address and utility setup as required by the city.
Final walkthrough.
Clean up and haul away debris.

notes: Client description did not specify tub vs. shower — scoped as a shower with hot-mop pan per the studio/family-member use case; confirm with client and adjust if a tub is preferred.
Kitchenette appliance supply (contractor-provided vs. client-provided) was not specified — line assumes contractor installs, confirm who purchases the appliances before finalizing pricing.
Assumed no existing plans are in hand, so a full architect/engineer plan set is included in scope; if the client already has approved plans, this line and its associated cost should be removed.
